chunks.title: Rabin-Karp String Matching Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The topic is Rabin Kaab algorithm. It's a pattern matching algorithm or string

chunks.title: Rabin-Karp String Matching Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: matching algorithm. The problem is if a text is given and a pattern is given

chunks.title: Rabin-Karp String Matching Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then we have to find out whether this pattern is present in this string or not

chunks.title: Rabin-Karp String Matching Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: or text or not. There are various algorithms for string matching or

chunks.title: Rabin-Karp String Matching Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: pattern matching. This is one of the pattern matching algorithm. Let us see

chunks.text: case and finally we will see the actual algorithm. Let us start.

text: The topic is Rabin Kaab algorithm. It's a pattern matching algorithm or string matching algorithm. The problem is if a text is given and a pattern is given then we have to find out whether this pattern is present in this string or not or text or not. There are various algorithms for string matching or pattern matching. This is one of the pattern matching algorithm. Let us see how it works. First I will discuss the basic idea of Rabin Kaab algorithm and what is the worst case of Rabin Kaab algorithm then how to improve the worst case and finally we will see the actual algorithm. Let us start. This is a text given and this is the pattern we have to find out. You can see that AAB is existing here inside the text and how do you check? We know the basic algorithm in which we will start comparing the alphabets. Here we don't want to compare each and every alphabet. If you take a pattern there are three alphabets. Instead of checking each and every alphabet can't we make it as a single value? How we can make it as a single value? We can convert this into some numeric value. Let us see how it is possible. If the letters if I take ABC alphabets then for the alphabets there are ASCII codes like small a lowercase a is 97 so we can use that number. We will use that number but if I use that number and explain you then the numbers will be bigger. To take small numbers just to understand the algorithm instead of 97 I'll give my own codes A let it be 1 and B as 2 and C as 3, D as 4 and so on. I have given codes for all the alphabets from A to J. 10 alphabets I have taken. If you want you can take ASCII codes also but this will be simple for discussing the topic. Here is a pattern now using those codes we will try to find out a single value for this pattern. I will use those values this is 1 and this is also 1 and this is 2. I will add these values this is 4 even if you want you can multiply also that is your wish. I have added these values so now whatever the value I got this I will call it as hash code and the procedure of getting that value that is the function we will call it as hash function of this pattern P. Let us say P is a pattern so we call this as a hash function we are using hash function and finding value of a hash function. Now this hash function we use it for finding this pattern inside this text. How to do that? As the size of this one is 3 so let us say M is size is 3 and the size of this one is N and this is 6. Now how to check this? I will take 3 letters that is depending on the size of this pattern 3 letters and what are their codes 1 plus 1 plus 1 and this is 3 but see this is hash function value is 4 and there it is 3 it means they are not matching I don't have to check the characters just I have used a function. Function means it will take constant time so using constant time I found out the value and it is 3 and this is 4 they are not matching so this pattern is not there. Then slide to the next set of 3 alphabets so I will remove this and I will extend this one plus this is also 1 so this is 3 now again this is not matching so take out this slide next now this is again 3 and 3 is not matching with this one means these 3 letters are not forming this pattern. Now slide next see this sliding is called as rolling hash function from the present value I am getting the next value so that is called as rolling hash function we will see it further and include this one so the code for this one is 2 so this is 4 they are matching once you get a match confirm really this pattern is there or not so now check the alphabets these are a a and these are also a these are b yes they are same so pattern is found from index 4 to index 6 in the main string that is text so this is the basic idea of Rabin Kaab algorithm.
